Revert DLL loading changes and streamline command handling for BC28 (#222)

This pull request introduces a new approach for handling PowerShell
command overrides for Business Central version 28 and above, while
maintaining the existing method for earlier versions. The main change is
the addition of a dynamic wrapper function generator
(`Invoke-PwshOverwriting`) that creates PowerShell Core wrappers for
specified commands, improving compatibility and parameter handling.
Several minor code style and consistency improvements are also included.

**Dynamic command overriding for BC 28+:**

* Added `Invoke-PwshOverwriting` function in
`Export-PwshCoreOverride.ps1` that dynamically generates wrapper
functions for specified command names, enabling better parameter
handling and serialization for PowerShell Core in Business Central
version 28 and above.
* Updated `NavAppManagement.ps1` to use `Invoke-PwshOverwriting` for BC
version 28 and above, and retained the legacy override export logic for
earlier versions.

**Code consistency and style improvements:**

* Standardized `param`/`dynamicparam` casing and improved code style in
`Export-PwshCoreOverride.ps1` for consistency with PowerShell best
practices.

commit.message:
fix: pre-load FFmpeg DLLs via LoadLibraryExW with absolute path

SetDllDirectoryW modifies the search order but the delay-load helper
can ignore it depending on how LoadLibraryEx is called internally.

New approach: LoadLibraryExW with the FULL absolute path to each DLL.
Windows caches modules by name, so when delay-load later calls
LoadLibrary('avcodec-61.dll') it finds the already-loaded module.
This is guaranteed to work regardless of search-path configuration.

Also use eprintln! before the logger is up so errors are always visible.

* fix: e2e cleanup functions — PostgREST compatibility + missing FK children

Bugs found during full E2E run (pre-existing, not from RBAC feature):

1. session_replication_role blocked by PostgREST — both e2e_cleanup_tenant
   and new e2e_delete_billing_records used SET session_replication_role which
   PostgREST denies. Replaced with ALTER TABLE DISABLE/ENABLE TRIGGER for
   each immutability trigger (billing_records, line_items, invoice_pdfs,
   aeat_submissions).

2. Missing FK child tables — e2e_cleanup_tenant didn't delete from
   billing_record_line_items, draft_line_items, delivery_note_items, or
   delivery_note_counters, causing silent FK constraint failures.

3. deleteBillingRecord() was nuclear — called e2e_cleanup_tenant which
   deletes the ENTIRE tenant. Replaced with targeted e2e_delete_billing_records
   that only removes billing data, keeping tenant/company/customers intact.

All 240 E2E tests pass. CI green (813 pytest + 238 vitest).

perf: strongly typed render plans + single-span renderer (#1306 #1307 #1308 #1309)

Move RenderPlan, RouteRenderPlans, RenderStats from Rendering → Core:
- BmwContext.CompiledPlans is now RouteRenderPlans? (no object? cast)
- RouteHandlerData.CompiledPlans is now RouteRenderPlans? (strongly typed)
- RenderStatsProvider in Core — no circular dependency

Single-span render path (#1309):
- RenderPlan.EstimateSize() precomputes exact output byte count
- RenderPlan.ExecuteSingleSpan() renders into one contiguous Span<byte>
- RenderWithPlansSingleSpanAsync: one GetSpan, one Advance, one Flush
- WriteHtmlEncodedToSpan uses UTF-8 byte literals (u8) for entities
- Maximum cache locality, minimum PipeWriter syscall overhead

Performance annotations (#1308):
- AggressiveInlining on Execute, ExecuteSingleSpan, Bind, EstimateSize
- AggressiveInlining on WriteHtmlEncoded, WriteSpan, WriteHtmlEncodedToSpan
- Span.CopyTo already uses SIMD via Buffer.Memmove at runtime

All 178 tests pass (176 rendering + 2 core).

Disable AIA certificate downloads for server client-cert validation by default (#125049)

During TLS handshake, peers should include all intermediate certificates
necessary for the other side to perform certificate validation. If
intermediates are not sent, it is possible for the receiver to look them
up and download using the Authority Information Access extension (AIA).

This behavior is not desirable on server side for multiple reasons:
- performance - AIA server may be slow or plain unresponsive, performing
network IO as part of certificate validation slows down the handshake.
- security - server makes outbound HTTP GET requests to client-provided
endpoint.

This PR changes the default behavior of server SslStream instances to
disable intermediate cert downloading using AIA by default. Scenarios,
where user passes custom `X509ChainPolicy` are not affected.

This change may break scenarios where client and/or server is not
properly configured, meaning any of:
- client does not send necessary intermediates in the handshake (on
Windows, this requires that the CA cert is present in the CA store)
- server does not have the necessary intermediate certificates in the CA
store.

Action to be taken by affected users are either:
- configure server auth options with `X509ChainPolicy` with `ExtraStore`
that contains the necessary intermediates.
- specify `X509ChainPolicy` that either allows AIA downloads (not
recommended)
- install CA certificates to the proper certificate store, so they can
be looked up when building the outbound cert chain (client) or when
validating client cert (server)

---------

commit.message:
feat: add Web Push notifications via Service Worker (works when website is closed)

Background push delivery so users receive notifications even when the
website tab is closed or the browser is in the background.

Infrastructure:
- Install minishlink/web-push (VAPID-based Web Push API)
- Generate VAPID key pair, stored in .env (VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY/PRIVATE_KEY)
- Add vapid_* keys to config/app.php
- Create push_subscriptions table (member_id, endpoint, p256dh, auth)
- Create PushSubscription model

Service layer:
- WebPushService: sendToMember() and sendToAll() with stale endpoint cleanup
- Wrap all push calls in try/catch so failures never block main flow

Server:
- NotificationController: subscribePush, unsubscribePush, vapidPublicKey endpoints
- GET /notifications/vapid-key (public, for SW registration)
- POST /notifications/push/subscribe, /unsubscribe (auth:member)

Client:
- public/sw.js: Service Worker that handles push events and notificationclick
  Shows native OS notification, opens /notifications on click
- notifications.js rewritten to combine SSE + Web Push:
  - Registers sw.js on page load
  - On permission grant: connects SSE (real-time while tab open)
    AND subscribes to Web Push (background delivery when closed)
  - urlBase64ToUint8Array helper for VAPID key conversion

Observers:
- PinjamanObserver: sends web push on loan verified + returned
- BukuObserver: broadcasts web push to all subscribed members on new book

Delivery matrix:
  Tab open        → SSE (~3s) + Web Push (backup)
  Tab background  → Web Push
  Website closed  → Web Push
  Browser closed  → not possible (OS limitation)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix infra change detection to use merge-base instead of base tip (#285)

The evaluation workflow was using a two-point diff (base_sha..head_sha) to
detect changed files, which includes files changed on main that the PR branch
hasn't merged yet. This caused false 'Infrastructure changes detected' triggers
when evaluation workflow files were modified on main after a PR branch diverged.

Switch to computing the merge-base first, so only changes actually introduced
by the PR are considered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: RenderPlan struct-of-arrays with ordinal binding (#1306)

Replace array-of-structs (RenderSegment[]) with struct-of-arrays:
parallel byte[] ops, byte[][] fragments, string[] tokenKeys, int[]
ordinals, bool[] isRawHtml arrays indexed by segment ordinal.

Bind() resolves token names to ordinal indices on first Execute():
- op 0: static fragment (direct copy)
- op 1/4: page value (ordinal into values[])
- op 2/5: app value (ordinal into appvalues[])
- op 3: byte-rendered value (ordinal into byteValues[])
- op 6: unresolved (dropped)

After bind, Execute() is pure switch + array[ordinal] — zero string
comparisons on the hot render path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
